essence: Operates computer‑controlled machining equipment to produce precision components safely and accurately.

identity.title: CNC Operator
identity.summary: Runs and monitors CNC machines, loads materials, adjusts settings, ensures dimensional accuracy, and maintains consistent production quality in manufacturing environments.

salary.notes: Operators with multi‑axis CNC experience, aerospace tolerances, or programming skills can exceed £48k-£60k+.

qualifications: Training in CNC machining, mechanical engineering, or manufacturing
Ability to operate CNC lathes, mills, or multi‑axis machines
Knowledge of tooling, feeds, speeds, and machining processes
Understanding of quality control, tolerances, and safety procedures

lifestyle.description: CNC operators work in manufacturing plants, precision engineering firms, and industrial workshops. They run machining equipment, monitor production, and ensure parts meet strict tolerances. The role suits individuals who enjoy precision work, machinery, and computer‑controlled systems.

habits: Operates CNC machines and monitors production
Loads materials and sets up tooling
Adjusts machine settings to maintain tolerances
